Import a Nectar collaboration bundle

Usage:
  nectar import [--config <path>] [--on-conflict <skip|overwrite|rename>] [--dry-run] [--json] <kind> <file>

Arguments:
  kind                Import kind. Currently only `page` is supported
  file                Path to a `nectar.page.v1` page collaboration bundle

End of options:
  Use `--` before positional values that start with `-`, for example `nectar new post -- --config`.

Options:
  -c, --config <path> Config path(s); repeat or comma-separate to deep-merge in order
  --on-conflict <skip|overwrite|rename>  How to handle existing page files: skip (default), overwrite, or rename
  --dry-run           Validate the bundle and report planned writes without changing files
  -j, --json          No-op here; `import page` always emits a JSON result. Accepted so the global `--json` flag does not error
  -h, --help          Show help for this command

Environment variables:
  Every flag has an env var fallback (CLI flag > env var > .nectarrc > config > default).
  Naming: NECTAR_<COMMAND>_<FLAG> (uppercased, dashes become underscores).
  Example: --config → NECTAR_IMPORT_CONFIG

Repeated flags:
  Scalar string flags use the last value.
  List-style string flags accumulate as comma-separated values in argv order.
  Boolean flags use the last positive or negated spelling.

Examples:
  nectar import page about.page.json --dry-run
  nectar import page about.page.json --on-conflict rename
  nectar import page about.page.json --on-conflict overwrite
